item.title: Clayton faces questions about election integrity, vows to enhance trust in DNI
item.description: Clayton, currently the U.S. attorney for the Southern District of New York, is expected to succeed Bill Pulte, Trump's controversial acting director of national intelligence.

item.title: Blanche reiterates "anti-weaponization" fund is "dead" at confirmation hearing
item.description: "It is a moot issue, meaning there is no weaponization fund. The weaponization fund is dead," Blanche told lawmakers on the Senate Judiciary Committee.

item.title: Todd Blanche's opening statement at confirmation hearing touts Trump administration successes
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche delivered his opening statement at the Senate Judiciary Committee hearing to vet him to permanently fill the role in the Trump administration.

item.title: Watch Live: Blanche testifies at confirmation hearing for attorney general
item.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche appearing before the Senate Judiciary Committee for his confirmation hearing to lead the Justice Department in a permanent capacity.

item.title: How ICE policy changes could impact the 2026 midterm elections
item.description: A man was fatally shot by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent in Biddeford, Maine, during a traffic stop near his home, prompting the agency to make certain policy changes. CBS News' Fin Gomez has more.

item.title: Seal pup shot in head, third incident in less than year, hospital says
item.description: A three-week-old male harbor seal is in critical but stable condition and undergoing treatment at a Washington state wildlife center.

item.title: More details emerge on D.C.-area ground stop that left thousands of travelers stranded
item.description: A Tuesday ground stop in the Washington, D.C., area over security concerns linked to Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi's visit left tens of thousands of travelers stranded. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi has more.

item.title: How much interest can a $250,000 1-year CD account earn now?
item.description: A deposit that large can generate hefty interest returns, but where you keep it plays a big role in what you earn.

item.title: U.S. announces new strikes on Iran as war intensifies
item.description: The U.S. announces new strikes on Iran just hours after a 4th consecutive nighttime barrage, as the war ramps back up over control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Wildfires expose millions in Midwest, Northeast to dangerous smoke
item.description: Heavy smoke from several large wildfires blazing in Canada and Minnesota is expected to engulf large swaths of the Midwest and Northeast U.S. this week.

item.title: Will daylight saving time become permanent after House passes bill?
item.description: Lawmakers are one step closer to making daylight saving time permanent​ after the House passed a bill with overwhelming support.

item.title: U.S. Mint to make $1 gold coin with Trump's face on it
item.description: It's the latest effort by President Trump to make his mark on Washington, D.C.

item.title: Grizzlies | 60 Minutes Archive
item.description: From 2020, Bill Whitaker reports on the resurgence of the once-endangered grizzly bear population in the United States.

item.title: Why is Zelenskyy shaking up his cabinet amid successes against Russia?
item.description: Ukraine's president is shaking up his government just as observers note positive changes in the trajectory of the war with Russia.

item.title: "Miracle on the Hudson" pilot, Capt. "Sully," shares Alzheimer's diagnosis
item.description: Capt. Chesley "Sully" Sullenberger, who famously landed a passenger jet on the Hudson River in 2009, said he has been diagnosed with Alzheimer's disease.

item.title: How much money can a smart thermostat realistically save during a heat wave?
item.description: A smart thermostat can lower cooling costs, but your actual savings depend on how you use it during extreme heat.

item.title: Spain dominates France 2-0, advancing to the World Cup final
item.description: Spain stunned France 2-0 in their semifinal match, lining up one half of the World Cup final. CBS Sports Golazo Network analyst Charlie Davies comments on Spain's decisive victory and looks ahead to the Argentina-England semifinal match.

item.title: Inside look at the talk show "Better in Person"
item.description: Author and journalist Stephen Dubner, who rose to fame when he co-wrote The New York Times bestseller "Freakonomics," talks with "CBS Mornings" about launching his own weekly talk show on YouTube, "Better in Person." Dubner discusses how the show got its start, his wide range of guests and why he decided to self-finance it.

item.title: Video shows aftermath of fatal ICE shooting in Maine
item.description: Security video appears to show the moments after immigration agents fatally shot a man in Maine. CBS News' Lilia Luciano and Nicole Sganga have more.

item.title: Family says they're forced to sell home to help power data centers
item.description: Georgia Power says building a new transmission line will require acquiring more than 300 parcels of land, including residential properties.

item.title: The midair refueling pilots that keep U.S. military operations going around the world
item.description: Many U.S. military operations rely on the work of pilots flying midair refueling tankers, including the KC-135 Stratotanker. Charlie D'Agata visited U.S. forces stationed at a Royal Air Force base in Mildenhall, England, to learn about "the gas stations of the sky."

item.title: Trump defends ICE efforts during traffic stops after fatal shootings in Maine and Texas
item.description: Every ICE arrest team will have at least one officer wearing a body camera, the Department of Homeland Security announced following two fatal shootings by ICE agents who were not wearing bodycams. President Trump defended ICE's operations on social media amid the policy changes. Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more details.

item.title: Subaru recalls more than 541,000 vehicles over labeling error
item.description: Erroneous labels on some Subaru Crosstrek, Forester and Ascent cars could increase the risk of a crash, NHTSA said.

item.title: ICE halts most vehicle stops and increases bodycam use after fatal shootings in Texas and Maine
item.description: Every ICE arrest team will have at least one officer wearing a body camera, the Department of Homeland Security announced following two fatal shootings by ICE agents who were not wearing bodycams. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez has more details.

item.title: House passes bill to make daylight saving time permanent
item.description: The measure also allows states to opt out if they take action before the federal law is enacted.

item.title: Squeeze what? South Florida woman gets surprising license plate
item.description: Pompano Beach resident Nancy Dello Stritto, who is almost 77 years old, opened her mail to find a license plate reading "SQZ A55."

item.title: Introducing Norah O'Donnell's new health podcast
item.description: "Healthful with Norah O'Donnell" launches July 22.

item.title: Alzheimer's blood test may show who's likely to develop it in 5 to 10 years
item.description: A blood test may predict if apparently healthy older adults are likely to develop Alzheimer's symptoms in the next five or 10 years, researchers say.

item.title: Flights grounded at D.C.-area airport due to security concerns for Iraqi leader
item.description: The Iraqi leader was in Washington to meet with President Trump.

item.title: Darline Graham, Lindsey Graham's sister, sworn in as senator
item.description: Darline Graham, the sister of the late Sen. Lindsey Graham, was sworn in to serve out the remainder of his term in the Senate, following his sudden death over the weekend.
